summary: Iteration 2 testing: all 25 backend pytest cases PASS (9 new + 16 regression). Frontend E2E covers 13/13 new flows PASS: forgot-password, reset-password (renders), public 404 page, signup, navbar (Resumes/Letters/Settings), settings save profile, cover-letter create, template switch, Quick AI modal open, share toggle for cover letter (with public URL + public /c/{slug} view, no edit UI), share toggle for resume (with public URL + public /r/{slug} view, no edit UI), logout. Static SEO files /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml return 200. AdSense fallback placeholder renders correctly. Found and fixed 1 production bug in cover-letter creation (DuplicateKeyError on public_slug:null with sparse unique index).
action_items: Bug fixed in /app/backend/server.py: removed `public_slug: None` from create_cover_letter doc — sparse-unique index in MongoDB still indexes nulls, causing DuplicateKeyError on the second cover-letter insert. Fix verified via pytest. Note: an existing legacy document with public_slug:null was unset directly in MongoDB to unblock testing. In production rollouts, run a one-time migration: db.cover_letters.updateMany({public_slug:null}, {$unset:{public_slug:''}})
critical_code_review_comments: RESOLVED bug: server.py create_cover_letter inserted public_slug:None alongside a sparse unique index — sparse only excludes documents missing the field, NOT null values. Second insert always hit E11000 DuplicateKeyError.

server.py /ai/cover-letter: JSON-parse fallback wraps the whole raw response into body[0] (truncated to 600 chars) — could mask LLM format issues silently. Consider logging at warning level on parse failure (same pattern as /ai/score from iteration 1).
server.py DELETE /auth/account: cascades resumes + cover_letters but does NOT delete password_reset_tokens for that user. Low risk (TTL index drops them anyway in 1 hour) but could be tightened.
CoverLetterBuilder.jsx auto-save fires on every change without debouncing visible in code; ensure useEffect debounce is in place to avoid hammering /api/cover-letters/{id} PUT.

robots.txt currently begins with a Cloudflare-style 'content signals' preamble — verify this is intentional content from the owner; otherwise replace with a standard User-agent / Disallow directive set.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@resumeforge.ai / Admin@12345 (seeded). E2E test users created with pattern e2e_*_<ts>@example.com and TEST_*_<uuid>@resumeforge.ai during pytest.
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates ~6 ephemeral users (TEST_*) plus 2-3 cover letters/resumes; CRUD tests delete their own data; delete_account test cascades user + resumes + cover_letters. Frontend E2E creates 3-4 e2e_* users (no auto-cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/ is split into backend_test.py (iteration 1) and test_iteration2.py (iteration 2). Both run cleanly together: 25 passed in ~29s. The cover-letter share endpoint POST /cover-letters/{id}/share returns {is_public, public_slug}. Public read endpoints /api/share/resume/{slug} and /api/share/cover-letter/{slug} require no auth and 404 when is_public=false. AI cover letter endpoint takes 5-15s — use timeout>=60s. Frontend testids: dashboard 'dashboard-create-resume-button'/'empty-create-button', cover letters 'cover-letter-create-button'/'cover-empty-create', share buttons 'share-toggle-resume'/'share-toggle-cover-letter' with popovers 'share-link-popover-{kind}' (only renders when is_public=true), logout 'nav-logout-button'. AdSense placeholder shows 'SPONSORED · AD' striped block when REACT_APP_ADSENSE_CLIENT_ID is unset.
rca of the issue: BUG: POST /api/cover-letters returned 500 Internal Server Error on the second insert. RCA: server.py:create_cover_letter wrote {..., public_slug: None, ...} into Mongo. The collection has `db.cover_letters.createIndex('public_slug', {unique:true, sparse:true})`. Sparse indexes only exclude documents that omit the field entirely; documents with explicit null ARE indexed. So the first cover-letter inserts {public_slug:null} and consumes the unique-null slot; the second insert hits E11000 DuplicateKeyError. Reproduction: create 2 cover letters as the same user → second returns 500. Fix applied: removed the `public_slug: None` line from the insert payload (slug is added later by /share endpoint). Migration for any existing legacy nulls: db.cover_letters.updateMany({public_slug:null},{$unset:{public_slug:''}}). Re-tested: TestCoverLetters::test_cover_letter_full_crud and TestProfile::test_delete_account_user_succeeds_and_cascades both PASS.
